title: Explaining ICT Macros (xx:50 to xx:10)
author: macr0trader
selftext:
**What is a macro?**  
\-A macro is a time window beetween xx:50 to xx:10 of every hour and its a function in the market.

\-When that time starts, the price will do a specific thing, in this case, will go to a PDArray (Like a point of interest) the market is willing to go before moves the next liquidity.

**Why ICT Macros should be a important factor for you?**  
\-I see a lot of people in front of the charts for hours and hours for nothing...Maybe they make some profit, but then they end the day negative. Thats not wealthy.

\-Thats where the macro enters. Because its a time window you can use to make trades, everything outside that time window, you dont care!

**What PDArray i should use in that time window?**

\-In my opinion...Inversion fair value gap is the best way to use this. Of course you have more. Also...you need to know the next draw on liquidity. Thats not so hard to be honest...

ICT said very clear for all of us, **TIME AND PRICE.**

**First comes time, then...price.**

**What this means?**

\-You dont care where the price is before the macro. After macro starts, the price needs to be in sync with the PDArray

Look at the example i gave you, look how the price waited for the macro to hit the inversion fair value gap before explode to the buyside liquidiy...

Thats the pure algo working, **like a clock**. :D
